subject: UX & Research
title: Diary Studies
description: A quick win: understand Diary Studies.

hook.text: Ask users to log their experiences over two weeks. Day 1 is excitement. Day 7 is habit. Day 14 is the truth.

buildup.text: Diary studies capture longitudinal behavior that one-time tests miss. They show how usage evolves over time.

discovery.text: A fitness app might test great in a lab. But a diary study reveals that 70% of users stop by day 10.

twist.text: Diary fatigue is real. Participants stop logging carefully after one week. Keep prompts short and easy.

climax.text: Combine diaries with analytics. Self-reported data shows context and emotion; analytics show actual behavior.

punchline.text: One test shows first impressions. A diary shows the real relationship.

quiz.question: What do diary studies reveal that single-session tests miss?
options: How usage and attitudes evolve over time as novelty wears off
